doc_intro_p1: In a digital environment saturated with content generated or assisted by artificial intelligence, transparency is no longer optional—it is a guarantee of trust. The TCP/UP protocol (Transparent Content Protocol / User‑Powered) makes it possible to clearly declare the role of humans and AI in the production of content, in a simple, readable, and responsible way.
doc_intro_p2: This guide walks you step by step through applying the protocol, from analyzing your creative process to integrating visual and technical signatures, including choosing the correct label.
doc_intro_warning: Important: TCP/UP is a declarative transparency protocol regarding the use of AI. It is neither technical proof, third‑party certification, nor legal advice. Its purpose is to make the editorial process clearer for the public.
doc_intro_note: Note: This charter is a civic initiative based on good‑faith declaration and author responsibility.
doc_s1_heading: 1. Analyzing your creative process – The decision tree (the 30‑second rule)
doc_s1_intro: Before applying a label, determine where your work falls on the human / AI spectrum using this logical flow. Need help? "/pages/wizard.html"Find your label in 30 seconds with the selection assistant
doc_s1_q1: 1. Did AI generate, rephrase, or correct any part of the content?
doc_s1_q1_li1: NO → [TCP/UP: HUC] (no AI used)
doc_s1_q1_li2: YES → go to question 2
doc_s1_q2: 2. Did AI modify the meaning or structure of ideas (rephrasing, restructuring, adding new ideas)?
doc_s1_q2_li1: NO (limited to spelling/grammar) → [TCP/UP: HCA]
doc_s1_q2_li2: YES → go to question 3
doc_s1_q3: 3. Did the substance of the content (intent, structure, argumentation) remain entirely human‑designed?
doc_s1_q3_li1: Yes, the substance is human and AI only helped with wording or structure → [TCP/UP: HCE]
doc_s1_q3_li2: No, AI contributed to the outline, ideas, angle, or structure → go to question 4
doc_s1_q4: 4. Was the content co‑constructed with AI or primarily generated by it?
doc_s1_q4_li1: Co‑construction with substantial human input → [TCP/UP: ACE]
doc_s1_q4_li2: Generation mostly delegated to AI, with limited human validation or selection → [TCP/UP: AIC]
doc_s1_variant: Variant: If you provided a very detailed prompt already describing the intellectual architecture, arguments, and tone, HCE may be considered. As a precaution, if AI nonetheless influenced final organization, introduced new angles, or guided the structure of the text, ACE remains preferable.
doc_s1_prudence: Principle of caution: If you hesitate between two labels, choose the one that gives the greatest share to AI. Transparency outweighs pride.
doc_s2_huc_def: No AI used in the creation process
doc_s2_huc_use: text, image, or document created without AI assistance
doc_s2_hca_def: AI limited to spelling, grammar, or syntax correction
doc_s2_hce_def: Human substance, formulation or formatting assisted
doc_s2_ace_def: AI contributed to the substance alongside the human
doc_s2_aic_def: Content mostly or entirely generated by AI
doc_s2_aic_use: raw generation, automatic summary, unreviewed translation
doc_s2_example_huc: HUC: a literary manuscript typed without any assistance, an editorial where every word is manually chosen, a digital drawing made by hand without AI filters.
doc_s2_example_hca: HCA: a thesis proofread by an AI corrector without style change, a legal contract checked for typos.
doc_s2_example_hce: HCE: meeting notes turned into a structured report, an internal email rephrased to be more diplomatic.
doc_s2_example_ace: ACE: an SEO article generated 50% by AI then rewritten and enriched with human expertise, a business plan whose structure was provided by AI and filled in by a human.
doc_s2_example_aic: AIC: a prompt‑generated logo, a Bash script created entirely by AI, raw translation of a technical manual.
doc_s2_cas_particuliers: Special cases: For more ambiguous situations (translation, code, brainstorming, mega‑prompt…), consult the Critical cases and arbitration section (section 4 of the full documentation).
toc_s4_2_3: 4.2.3 AI‑generated content heavily restructured by a human
toc_s4_2_4: 4.2.4 AI brainstorming and autonomous human writing
toc_s4_2_7: 4.2.7 Publication without full review (“Blind Output” case)
toc_s4_4: 4.4 Technical granularity of the ACE label
